page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-72 is a general-purpose server instance configured with the x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 72 dedicated vCPUs and 288.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This hardware profile is designed to support workloads requiring consistent, non-shared compute resources without the need for specialized hardware accelerators.

This instance type does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. Additionally, the configuration does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The absence of local NVMe drives and GPU hardware represents a design tradeoff that prioritizes standard compute and memory density over specialized acceleration or high-speed local I/O.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4a-standard-72 offers a predictable resource allocation model for enterprise applications, web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks. It is structured for workloads such as application servers, containerized microservices, and general-purpose database hosts that benefit from dedicated CPU execution but do not demand high-performance local storage or graphics processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-72 is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 72 dedicated vCPUs and 288.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Positioned as a general-purpose resource, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation to ensure consistent performance for multi-threaded applications. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring users to rely on network-attached storage for data persistence. It also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the c4a-standard-72 offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise workloads, web serving, and containerized deployments where specialized hardware acceleration is unnecessary. This configuration is best suited for general-purpose computing, application hosting, and moderate database workloads.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring seventy-two dedicated virtual cores and two hundred eighty-eight gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.
